title: transcript::current_git_branch spawns a blocking git subprocess in async hot path
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/transcript.rs:113-128, 132-149 (current_git_branch + envelope_for)
## Violation
```rust
pub fn current_git_branch(cwd: &Path) -> Option<String> {
    let out = std::process::Command::new("git")
        .args(["rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"])
        .current_dir(cwd)
        .output()
        .ok()?;
    ...
}
```

`envelope_for` is called for every appended transcript line, and `current_git_branch` is invoked unconditionally inside it. This spawns a blocking `git` subprocess synchronously on whatever thread is running. When the caller is a tokio task (proxy.rs and ACP both wrap appends in async handlers), this blocks the executor thread for the duration of `git rev-parse` — typically 5-50 ms but can be seconds on a cold cache or large repo.

The contract claim in the doc comment ("takes longer than 2 seconds") is wrong: `std::process::Command::output()` has no timeout. A wedged git lock would hang the call indefinitely; the comment misleads operators reading the code.
## Mandated refactor
Cache the branch per (`cwd`, tokio mtime of `.git/HEAD`) — most appends in a session see the same branch. Move the git invocation behind `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` when called from async context, OR invalidate the cache only when `.git/HEAD` mtime changes. Either way, delete the misleading "2 seconds" claim from the doc comment OR add a real timeout via `wait_timeout` / `tokio::process::Command` with `select!`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
